news.summary: Hyperliquid, a decentralized perpetuals exchange, submitted two comment letters to the CFTC on perpetual derivatives and 24/7 trading. The CFTC is proactive on these topics and plans to approve crypto perpetual futures contracts soon. Hyperliquid aims to advance DeFi, implementing core principles for market efficiency and user protection. The crypto derivatives market is active, with Coinbase acquiring Deribit, Gemini expanding crypto derivatives trading in Europe, and Synthetix venturing into crypto derivatives. CFTC Commissioner Mersinger supports bringing this trading back to the US. Perpetual futures contracts are open-ended derivatives tied to spot market prices through a funding rate mechanism.
news.headline: Hyperliquid submits CFTC comments on 24/7 derivatives trading, commending proactive engagement on market evolution. Source: Hyperliquid. US to approve crypto perpetual futures contracts 'very soon.' CFTC Commissioner encourages onshore trading. Perpetual futures allow price speculation without asset ownership, with no expiration dates. Crypto derivatives market active with product launches, acquisitions, and regulations across Europe and the Americas. Coinbase acquires Deribit, Gemini expanding in Europe, Synthetix exploring crypto options. TradFi building Ethereum L2s for tokenizing RWAs.

These changes have made it more difficult for small business owners to secure the necessary resources for their ventures.
news.headline: Under the current administration, the Small Business Administration faces challenges with reduced staff and tighter loan terms, impacting entrepreneurs.

news.summary: The global hair growth supplements market was valued at approximately USD 940.1 million in 2025, with a projected increase to around USD 2,581.1 million by 2032, showing a robust compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 15.5%. This expansion is driven by the preference for nutraceuticals over pharmaceuticals, rising awareness of supplement benefits, increasing hair loss cases, and a focus on healthy hair maintenance. Single ingredient supplements are expected to dominate in 2025, with capsules being the most popular formulation, and hair regrowth functionality being prominent. North America leads the market, with Asia Pacific showing the fastest growth. The rise in hair loss disorders globally, like androgenetic alopecia and alopecia areata, is propelling market growth. However, factors like high product costs and availability of alternative therapies may hinder market expansion. The full article can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Global hair growth supplements market valued at USD 940.1M in 2025, projected to reach USD 2,581.1M by 2032 with 15.5% CAGR. North America leads, Asia Pacific to see fastest growth due to rising hair loss cases & focus on healthy hair. High costs & alternative treatments may limit market growth.

news.summary: Senator Elizabeth Warren strongly criticized President Donald Trump's $3.8 trillion tax package following the narrow approval by the House in a 215-214 vote. She accused billionaires Jeff Bezos and Elon Musk of celebrating the bill's passage while millions are facing cuts to healthcare. Warren stated that the bill is a major tax break for the wealthy, financed by stripping health insurance from 14 million individuals. The legislation will now move to the Senate for consideration before the July 4 recess. This development caused a significant reaction in the bond market, with 30-year Treasury yields surging to 5.15%—the highest level since October 2023—as investors seek increased compensation for heightened fiscal risk. Warren's remarks and further details can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Elizabeth Warren criticizes Trump's $3.8 trillion tax bill, House narrowly passes it 215-214. Bezos and Musk cheer while millions face healthcare cuts. 30-year Treasury yields spike to 5.15%.

news.summary: On May 23, 2025, in Shenzhen, China, the 3rd Generation Partnership Project (3GPP) declared that Dr. Wang Ruixin from vivo was awarded the 3GPP Excellence Award 2024. This recognition was bestowed upon him during the 3GPP RAN4#115 gathering in Malta on May 19. Dr. Wang Ruixin is recognized as a prominent figure in the telecommunications field, and this accolade signifies his remarkable contributions to the industry.
news.headline: Dr. Wang Ruixin from vivo wins 3GPP Excellence Award for contributions to 5G OTA standards at meeting in Malta.

news.summary: An initial assessment of effectiveness from the elacestrant combined with everolimus and ribociclib groups in the ELEVATE trial shows promising results. In addition, new safety information from other groups in the study involving elacestrant paired with targeted therapies will also be shared. These findings underscore the potential of elacestrant in treating the condition. The research will showcase the impact of these combinations on improving outcomes, with specific figures and insights to be revealed during the presentation.
news.headline: Menarini Group to present updated data on elacestrant combinability in ER+, HER2- mBC at ASCO 2025, including efficacy and safety outcomes from various combination cohorts.

news.summary: KBRA has issued its European Auto ABS Indices for April 2025, showcasing the latest credit trends in securitised auto loans and leases. The report offers insights for investors looking to navigate this market. KBRA, a prominent credit rating agency, operates in the U.S., EU, and the UK, and holds the status of Qualified Rating Agency in Taiwan. Additionally, it is a designated Rating Organization for structured finance ratings in Canada. With a broad reach, KBRA serves as a valuable resource for investors seeking reliable credit ratings.
news.headline: KBRA releases European Auto ABS Indices for April 2025, offering credit trends for securitised auto loans. Check report for details.

news.summary: Bitcoin buyer interest is at all-time highs, with the price uptrend expected to continue according to CryptoQuant analysis. Bitcoin short-term holders are seeing profits as the market surges, with buy orders dominating and sellers ceding control. The 90-day cumulative volume delta (CVD) favors Bitcoin bulls, indicating potential for further upward movement. Hodlers are holding off on selling, with daily profit-taking lower compared to when Bitcoin first hit $100,000 in 2024. Older coins are less active, showing stronger holding behavior. Price momentum increased after reclaiming the average cost basis for Bitcoin's short-term holders at just under $100,000. Bitcoin is rallying, and readers are advised to conduct their own research before making investment decisions.
news.headline: Bitcoin buyer dominance at $111K signals potential for further gains as CryptoQuant analysis suggests uptrend may continue. BTC holders refrain from selling at current levels, with older coins showing stronger holding behavior. Price momentum increases after reclaiming average cost basis for short-term holders at just under $100K.

news.summary: Anton Bukov, co-founder of decentralized exchange 1inch, highlighted that traditional banks spend $100-$300 per user for onboarding, while online banks spend $20-$30. DeFi, on the other hand, requires minimal costs, giving it an advantage in reaching the 1.4 billion unbanked people globally. Bukov emphasized that DeFi allows the unbanked to access the global economy easily using stablecoins like Tether. He expressed that DeFi serves as a tool for financial inclusion and provides access to global liquidity. Bukov mentioned that countries aligning regulations with this new economic trend can benefit from capital movement and cooperation.
news.headline: DeFi enables 1.4B unbanked access with minimal costs; traditional banks spend $100-$300 per user, DeFi requires near-zero onboarding expense.

news.summary: Learn more about Moldova, a wine nation located in CHIȘINĂU. In a significant development, the World Congress of Vine and Wine will be held in Moldova for the first time in 2025. Moldova boasts the highest vineyard area per capita globally, with an impressive 4 hectares per person. This event signifies Moldova's prominent position in the wine industry and offers unique insights into the country's viticulture practices.
news.headline: Moldova to host 46th World Congress of Vine and Wine, known for highest vineyard density per capita (4 hectares).

news.summary: Tom Erik Foss-Jacobsen has been named the new CEO of Borregaard ASA, taking over from Per A. Sørlie who is retiring after holding the position for 26 years, as announced on December 3, 2020, to the Oslo Stock Exchange. Foss-Jacobsen will assume the role on August 1, 2025.
news.headline: Tom Erik Foss-Jacobsen named CEO of Borregaard ASA, succeeding Per A. Sørlie after 26 years. Transition effective 1 August 2025.

news.summary: In the recent financial report, MINISO mainland China experienced a notable revenue increase of 18.9% compared to the previous year. The March Quarter demonstrated a substantial sequential growth in same-store sales. The gross margin also showed a positive trend, reaching 44.2%, an increase of 0.8 percentage points year over year. Additionally, the adjusted EBITDA surged by 7.5% from the previous year, totaling RMB1,037.3 million. Overall, shareholder returns saw a promising uptrend.
news.headline: MINISO Group's revenue grew by 18.9% to RMB1,037.3 million in March Quarter 2025 with gross margin reaching 44.2%. Adjusted EBITDA increased by 7.5% year over year.

news.summary: Investors are currently analyzing the potential effects of the "One Big Beautiful Bill Act," but an expert is cautioning them to also consider the U.S. tariff policy. Bob Elliott, the CIO of Unlimited Funds, believes that the stock market is not fully pricing in the risks associated with tariff rates and argues that understanding the intricacies of this policy is crucial. Despite recent progress with a "China deal," Elliott notes that tariff rates remain high, raising questions about whether further relief is expected or if the U.S. will revert to higher tariffs. The economic consequences of these decisions could vary significantly. This insight was shared in a post on May 22, 2025, and the full story can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Expert Bob Elliott warns stock market is underpricing tariff risks, says nuances more crucial than legislative package. Despite recent China deal, tariff rates still high, uncertain. Economic impacts could be significant.

news.summary: The latest data shows that as of today, there have been a total of 250,000 confirmed cases of COVID-19 in the country. Out of these cases, 50,000 have resulted in hospitalization, with 10,000 requiring intensive care. The death toll stands at 5,000. The government has implemented various measures to combat the spread of the virus, including mandatory mask-wearing in public spaces and restrictions on large gatherings.Officials are urging the public to continue practicing social distancing and proper hygiene to help flatten the curve.
news.headline: Decisions at the AGM of AS Ekspress Grupp on the 23rd May 2025 were approved by shareholders.

news.summary: The European Union's Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) regulation sets stringent rules for stablecoin issuers in the EU. To trade in the EU, stablecoin issuers must acquire a license to become an electronic money institution, hold 60% of reserves in EU-based banks, provide full transparency through disclosures, and comply with other regulations. Tether, a significant stablecoin issuer with its USDT, rejected MiCA regulations citing concerns about banking regulations, distrust in the digital euro, and prioritizing users in countries like Brazil, Turkey, and Nigeria. Exchanges like Binance and Kraken have delisted USDT trading pairs for users in the European Economic Area due to non-compliance, affecting liquidity and forcing users towards MiCA-compliant stablecoins like USDC and EURC. The move by Tether to avoid MiCA highlights the regulatory challenges in the global crypto landscape, with companies resorting to regulatory arbitrage by moving to friendlier jurisdictions. Tether is now focusing on El Salvador and diversified investments after rejecting MiCA, illustrating the complex dynamics of global crypto regulations and business operations.
news.headline: Tether resists MiCA for banking, user privacy, and global impact reasons. EU rules reshape crypto space with 60% bank reserves mandate. Exchanges delist USDT in EEA due to non-compliance. Tether finds new opportunities in El Salvador and tech investments amid regulatory challenges. Regulatory arbitrage in crypto industry underlines disjointed global standards. Tether's decision showcases the struggle of complying with varying regulations worldwide.

news.summary: In Singapore on May 23, 2025, RightShip is committed to helping customers achieve Zero Harm in the maritime industry by ensuring no harm comes to vessels, people, and the environment. They utilize unique data and insights to support this mission. Their focus is on enhancing safety and sustainability across the global maritime value chain.

news.summary: Swedish crypto asset manager Virtune has listed its flagship Virtune XRP ETP on Germany's Deutsche Börse Xetra, expanding its digital asset offerings into Europe's largest economy. Since its establishment in May 2023, Virtune has grown rapidly in the Nordics, listing 16 products and attracting over 140,000 investors. The Virtune XRP ETP, initially listed on Nasdaq Stockholm, has garnered 50,000 investors and over $125 million in assets under management. Coinbase serves as the custodian for the product, ensuring institutional-grade security. Virtune aims to provide regulated access to crypto assets through ETPs, enhancing transparency and investor protection. CEO Christopher Kock highlights the strategic importance of the Xetra listing for Virtune's European expansion and emphasizes the strong demand for XRP as a leading global digital asset.
news.headline: Swedish Virtune launches XRP ETP on Deutsche Börse Xetra, expanding into Germany; over 140,000 investors, $125M AUM.

news.summary: In Sandviken, Sweden, on May 23, 2025, Alleima's Kanthal division, in collaboration with Danieli, has been chosen to provide a pilot-scale electric process gas heater for Emsteel's Direct Reduced Iron (DRI) facility in Abu Dhabi, UAE. The goal is to introduce electric heating to a portion of the current system. This initiative signifies a move towards sustainability and innovation in the industry. This project is a significant step towards reducing carbon emissions and modernizing traditional heating methods. The heater is set to be a crucial component in the plant's operations, marking a shift towards greener practices.
news.headline: Alleima's Kanthal division and Danieli to supply first electric process gas heater for commercial use in DRI; to be installed at Emsteel's DRI plant in UAE.
